question.content: From the calorimetry temperature-time curve, use back extrapolation of the cooling segment to the reaction start time to obtain the corrected temperature rise DeltaT. Output exactly one decimal number with 2 decimal places, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text. Answer format: a decimal number rounded to two decimal places; write only the numerical value itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Mark the reaction start time and extrapolate the post-peak cooling line back to that time.

step_3.content: Use the extrapolated temperature difference to get DeltaT.
step_3.key_point: The extrapolated temperature difference to get DeltaT.
